title: Dalton’s Fate Shakes Drum
description: On pages 14–16, dalton’s fate shakes drum as luffy reaches Dr. Kureha’s castle and meets Chopper, while the people below continue resisting Wapol’s return.
scene: Dalton’s Fate Shakes Drum. Luffy reaches Dr. Kureha’s castle and meets Chopper, while the people below continue resisting Wapol’s return. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Thanks for your help, but I'm fine as long as my fever's going down.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: My body can heal on its own now, right?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Don't go understanding a normally fatal disease now, you naive little girl. It'd normally take someone 10 whole days to recover with the proper treatment.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So unless you feel like experiencing a world of pain again before dying, I'd suggest you stay put.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Besides, with my medicine, you'll fully recover in just 3 more days!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: 3 whole days!? No, I have somewhere I need to be right n-

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: There're only two cases in which I let patients leave my sight. Hee hee hee hee hee hee hee hee...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: When they've healed... or when they've died!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You're not going anywhere, missy.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What...!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Gyaaaaa! Heeeelp!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Stop right there, you piece of meat!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hold your horses, Luffy. I'll first make sure to season it up first.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: If we're going to eat it, we might as well eat right, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Luffy, Sanji...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And... huh? Just what is that blue- nosed talking deer-doll?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Well, colour me shocked. Didn't think those two could be up and about so soon.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Who're they, chopper?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: He climbed up this mountain with his bare hands!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But Drum Rock's Peak is 500om tall!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: His body's completely frozen! Just what was he thinking, walking around without even a coat!?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Go warm up the bath and throw him in as soon as possible!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: This one's lost a lot of blood,

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Not to mention 6 broken ribs and a cracked spine...

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Is it alright if I operate on him?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: the worst one here is this girl. She's on the verge of death.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Chopper, go get me phenicol, cardiotonics, and some tialcillin!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Is she infected?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: ...yes, and I don't think it's a disease endemic to this island.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Shiver Shiver...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Don't worry.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Both of your pals here will be fine.

summary: This part centers on dalton’s fate shakes drum, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
